NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO BRING TO TRIAL; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; AND SUPPORTING DECLARATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100060105) FILED BY DEFENDANT TSE, HAI YAN TANG TSE, PAK YAT HEARING SET FOR FEB-21-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT HAI TSE, PAK TSE'S MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION FOR FAILURE TO BRING TO TRIAL. DEFENDANTS YAT AND TSE'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS ARE ESTOPPED FROM ASSERTING THE FIVE-YEAR DEADLINE. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 583.140; BORGLUND V. BOMBARDIER, LTD. (1981) 121 CAL.APP.3D 276, 281.) DEFENDANTS LULLED PLAINTIFF INTO THINKING ARBITRATION WOULD OCCUR, SOUGHT A CONTINUANCE OF THE ARBITRATION HEARING SET FOR OCTOBER 26, 2018, WITHIN THE FIVE-YEAR PERIOD, AND THEN ASSERTED THE DEADLINE. (SEE SCHMIDT DEC. 3; WANG DECL. 28.) THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO DISMISS THE ARBITRATION. (SEE BROCK V. KAISER FOUND. HOSPS. (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1790, 1797-1801.)ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Bring To Trial; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Supporting Declaration
Off Calendar Feb-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
DEan M. Schmidt, Esq., Telephone 415-296-1160, appearing for Plaintiff;
Nathan N. Wang, Esq., Carbone smoke Smith et al, Telephone 510-834-8450, appearing for DefendantPak Yat et al;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HAI TSE, PAK TSE's Motion To Dismiss Action For Failure To Bring To Trial.

Defendants Yat and Tse's motion to dismiss is denied. Defendants are estopped from asserting the five-year deadline. (See Code Civ. Proc. 583.140; Borglund v. Bombardier, Ltd. (1981) 121 Cal.App.3d 276, 281.) Defendants lulled plaintiff into thinking arbitration would occur, sought a continuance of the arbitration hearing set for October 26, 2018, within the five-year period, and then asserted the deadline. (See Schmidt Dec. 3; Wang Decl. 28.) The Court lacks jurisdiction to dismiss the arbitration. (See Brock v. Kaiser Found. Hosps. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1797-1801.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
